exact_quote: FunSearch (short for searching in the function space) combines a pretrained (frozen) LLM, whose goal is to provide creative solutions, with an evaluator, which guards against confabulations and incorrect ideas. FunSearch iterates over these two components, evolving initial low-scoring programs into high-scoring ones discovering new knowledge.
S: programs within a human-specified formalized problem
G: frozen pretrained LLM proposes program continuations
feedback_summary: evaluator scores feed evolutionary selection and later prompts
boundary: formalized task correctness does not establish field novelty or significance
uncertainty: no oracle claim; formal evaluate functions remain problem-bounded

exact_quote: In this spirit, we develop a recursive multidimensional symbolic regression algorithm that combines neural network fitting with a suite of physics-inspired techniques. We apply it to 100 equations from the Feynman Lectures on Physics, and it discovers all of them, while previous publicly available software cracks only 71; for a more difficult physics-based test set, we improve the state of the art success rate from 15% to 90%.
pi: recursive decomposition using symmetry and separability
evaluator_or_mechanism: recursive symbolic regression with neural fitting
feedback_summary: fit and structural tests guide recursive decomposition
boundary: no cross-problem memory or physical experiment loop is established
uncertainty: memory transfer and physical validation are not reported

exact_quote: By integrating 18 expert-designed tools, ChemCrow augments the LLM performance in chemistry, and new capabilities emerge. Our agent autonomously planned and executed the syntheses of an insect repellent, three organocatalysts, and guided the discovery of a novel chromophore. Our evaluation, including both LLM and expert assessments, demonstrates ChemCrow’s effectiveness in automating a diverse set of chemical tasks.
S: chemistry questions; synthesis plans; and tool calls
G: LLM agent plans and invokes chemistry tools
E: computational tools and reported physical syntheses
V: LLM and expert assessment are distinct mechanisms
A: plans; tool results; and synthesis artifacts
analysis_target: chemistry task performance and synthesis execution
evidence_source: tool results; physical execution; LLM assessment; expert assessment
feedback_summary: tool outputs inform subsequent agent actions within reported tasks
boundary: the abstract does not identify every routed validator edge or establish evaluator independence
uncertainty: LLM and expert assessment must not be collapsed into one calibrated channel

exact_quote: The energy of the filtered candidates is computed using DFT, both verifying model predictions and serving as a data flywheel to train more robust models on larger datasets in the next round of active learning.
G: graph networks generate or filter candidates
V: GNN prediction checks and DFT verification
A: candidate structures; energies; and model checkpoints
evidence_source: model predictions; DFT calculations; experimental realization reports
fidelity_boundary: computational surrogate with separate physical realization
feedback_summary: DFT results enter the next model-training round
boundary: DFT verification is not identical to synthesis or physical characterization
uncertainty: experimental realization is a separate evidential boundary

exact_quote: We externally validate the utility of pseudocode representations of text by generating accurate novel protocols using retrieved pseudocode, and we run a generated protocol successfully in our biological laboratory.
S: biology protocol descriptions and admissible pseudofunctions
V: automated pseudocode evaluation; scientist review; and laboratory outcome are three distinct records
A: protocol; pseudocode; review; and lab-result artifacts
decision_role: automated score; human review; one laboratory run
fidelity_boundary: protocol artifact to one physical laboratory run
feedback_summary: no recurring routed feedback path is established across the three records
boundary: one successful run and scientist review do not establish an independent calibrated gate or recurring physical cycle
uncertainty: do not call scientist review independent without a reported independence design

exact_quote: Structural causal models provide a language to state hypotheses, a blueprint for constructing LLM-based agents, an experimental design, and a plan for data analysis. The fitted structural causal model becomes an object available for prediction or the planning of follow-on experiments.
S: structural causal hypotheses over simulated scenarios
G: LLM proposes hypotheses and constructs agents
pi: scenario design and prospective follow-on planning
A: hypotheses; simulated data; and fitted model
analysis_target: causal hypotheses and simulated behavioral effects
evaluator_or_mechanism: structural causal model with LLM scientist and subjects
feedback_summary: fitted model is available for prediction or prospective planning
boundary: shared LLM mechanisms limit external correspondence; prospective planning is not a demonstrated repeated loop
uncertainty: real-human validity and executed follow-on feedback are not established

exact_quote: Despite the vastness of this space, evolutionary search can still discover two-layer neural networks trained by backpropagation. These simple neural networks can then be surpassed by evolving directly on tasks of interest, e.g. CIFAR-10 variants, where modern techniques emerge in the top algorithms, such as bilinear interactions, normalized gradients, and weight averaging.
S: programs built from basic mathematical operations
E: training and evaluation on computational tasks
evaluator_or_mechanism: evolutionary search over generic program space
feedback_summary: task fitness selects later program generations
boundary: benchmark performance does not establish interpretability or trustworthy scientific acceptance
uncertainty: search space and fitness remain human specified
